{{Quote|When he returns from wherever he has gone, I'm going to harshly reprimand him… then tell him about the time I ran into those mercenaries on Liaq.|Beryl Chiffonage, personal addendum to Rebel Task Force on Alliance Security SecuriDex file on Risiev Credal|<ref name="AIR" />}}
'''Liaq''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="AIR" /> located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Liaq system/Legends|Liaq system]].<ref name="TEA" /> At some point by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Beryl Chiffonage]] encountered a group of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] on Liaq. Later, when,<ref name="AIR" /> around 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> the Alliance officer [[Risiev Credal]] jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] during a patrol, with all contact with him being lost, he was considered to have potentially defected to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Chiffonage, who had mentored Credal, subsequently made a personal addendum to a [[Task Force on Alliance Security]] [[SecuriDex]] [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] on the latter officer. In the comment, Chiffonage noted that, when Credal returned,<ref name="AIR" /> he<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|123|Chiffonage, Beryl}}</ref> would reprimand him and then tell him about the encounter on Liaq.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Liaq was mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Liaq [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square M-11.<ref name="TEA" />
